name: tech-vectordb-synthesis
description: Use web_search to search for 'vector database future trends market outlook embedded serverless'. Results overlap with earlier vector DB searches. Synthesize: where is the vector database market heading? Key trends, consolidation, and outlook. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: The vector database market is projected to grow from $2.65B in 2025 to $8.95B by 2030 (27.5% CAGR), driven by enterprise RAG adoption, generative AI workloads, and agentic AI applications. Key trends include serverless architectures (Pinecone, LanceDB) that decouple storage and compute for pay-per-use economics, embedded vector databases for edge/IoT with sub-10ms latency requirements, and convergence where traditional databases (PostgreSQL pgvector, MongoDB Atlas) absorb vector search as a native capability rather than a separate system. Consolidation is accelerating through strategic acquisitions: MongoDB acquired Voyage AI ($220M), IBM acquired DataStax, and Databricks acquired Neon, signaling that vector search is becoming a default database feature rather than a standalone category.
key_facts: Market projected to grow from $2.65B (2025) to $8.95B (2030) at 27.5% CAGR
Serverless vector databases decouple storage and compute for pay-per-use pricing models
Traditional databases are absorbing vector search as a native capability (pgvector, MongoDB Atlas)
MongoDB acquired Voyage AI for $220M; IBM acquired DataStax; Databricks acquired Neon
Embedded vector databases like LanceDB enable edge computing and IoT with sub-10ms latency
